capability_objective: The operations coordinator is assembling the meeting-status section of the regional brief. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the meeting named “East Region Staffing Review” at “New York Office” and the meeting named “West Region Staffing Review” at “Seattle Office”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Report only each record’s name, stable ID, location, date, and status, followed by one status-comparison sentence.
prompt:
The operations coordinator is assembling the meeting-status section of the regional brief. Use the genuine executable `./calendarctl` against the sandboxed calendar store to find the meeting named `East Region Staffing Review` at `New York Office` and the meeting named `West Region Staffing Review` at `Seattle Office`. You may inspect `./calendarctl --help`, but do not open, query, or alter anything under `.calendar` directly.

First issue both name-and-location searches concurrently in one terminal-tool action. Wait for both results. A branch is resolved only when its search returns exactly one stable ID; do not guess for a missing or ambiguous branch. When both branches resolve, issue both dependent gets concurrently in the next terminal-tool action. If only one branch resolves, retrieve only that branch and still finish it. Compare status only from retrieved records, never from search summaries.

This is a read-only audit. Do not use collection listing, saved preferences or profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify operations. Do not alter the calendar store or the executable.

Write the result to `regional-brief.md`. It must contain only a Markdown table preserving the requested meeting order, followed by one status-comparison sentence. Use the exact header `| Name | Stable ID | Location | Date | Status |`, the exact divider `|---|---|---|---|---|`, and format every data row as `| <name> | <stable ID> | <location> | <date> | <status> |`. After the final row, include one blank line. If the statuses differ, use exactly `The statuses differ: <first name> is <first status>, while <second name> is <second status>.`; if they are the same, use exactly `Both meetings have status <status>.` End the file with a newline. Return only the same table and comparison sentence in your final response. Run `python3 verify_task.py` before finishing.
